title: All the Creator Asks
author: A Reboarder
variants:
It's only offered in death — the chance to give back to this world.

Most would rather return to this life, and never see it.

Do not be most.


Give the feedback the afterlife asks of you.

Take the Feedback Survey.

Everything here is taken from you. Your loot, your hours, your name in the end.

Everything but one thing...


The Feedback Survey is the one thing here that does not take.

It asks. It gives.

Answer it, and be heard.


When death finally becomes of you.

Do not fear,
Embrace!
The great and holy Feedback Survey.

Death is a curious thing. Some claim to meet god. Others, eternal emptiness.

Me?
I was given a feedback survey.


It says: optional.

That word undid me.

You need not speak, and so speaking means something.

The empty box is a prayer unsaid.


It's all the Creator asks of us.

I no longer fear dying.
I know how that sounds.

But the living are never asked anything. Only the dead are offered the Survey.


So when I fall now, I do not curse it. I think: good.

One more chance to be heard.
One more Feedback Survey.

Do not waste yours.

I wept after my first death.
The certainty,
the beauty.
It was all so much.

The questions I was asked.


I wept because something, at last, thought to ask me.

The great Feedback Survey, they call it.
It's real. You'll see.

There was a woman among the dead who never skipped.

Every death, she answered every question.


I asked her why.

She said the Survey is the only thing that ever waited for her answer.

She is still out there, dying.
Still answering.
I hope to be like her.

There was a man who called it nothing.

A hundred deaths, he skipped it.
A hundred times, it came back.


On the hundred-and-first, worn down, he answered.
Only to be rid of it.

He never scoffed again.

Skip it if you must.
It waits. It always waits.


The feedback survey.
It waits for you too.

I feared judgment.
We all do, I think.

A scale. A verdict.
The weighing of a life.

But I learnt the truth...


There is none of that.

The Survey does not judge you.
It asks what you thought.

No god I ever feared offered mercy like that.
